text:
Once upon a time in the serene village of Serenevale, there lived a humble blacksmith named Eliot. His workshop was nestled at the heart of the village, filled with the clang of hammers striking anvils and the warmth of firelight filtering through the large oak window. Eliot didn't receive many visitors; the villagers believed he wore the secrets of metal working like a cape.

One late spring afternoon, a gentle breeze carried a soft song off into the woods. Intrigued, Eliot ventured outside and heard a melodious voice. Following the sound, he discovered a beautiful fawn hopping through the meadows, its eyes reflecting the sun like embers. The fawn stopped near a puddle and looked up at Eliot with large, curious eyes.

"Hello," Eliot said softly. "You're not lost?"

The fawn nibbled on some grass before answering. "I don't think so. I've been here all morning. Who are you?"

'E
